sections.summary: Combining metoprolol with verapamil produces additive suppression of AV nodal conduction and cardiac contractility, risking severe bradycardia, heart block, or hypotension.
sections.mechanism: Metoprolol slows AV nodal conduction and reduces contractility through beta-1 blockade, while verapamil independently slows AV conduction and depresses contractility through calcium channel blockade. Verapamil also modestly inhibits metoprolol's CYP2D6-mediated metabolism, raising beta-blocker levels further.
sections.clinical_effects: Patients can develop marked bradycardia, high-grade AV block, hypotension, or worsening heart failure, particularly in those with underlying conduction disease or reduced left ventricular function.
sections.management: Use the combination cautiously and generally avoid it in patients with conduction abnormalities or significant left ventricular dysfunction; if both are needed, start at low doses and titrate slowly with close monitoring.
sections.monitoring: Obtain baseline and follow-up ECGs to assess heart rate and PR interval, and monitor blood pressure and symptoms of heart failure after initiation or dose changes.

evidence.citation: Calan (verapamil) FDA prescribing information, Drug Interactions section
